name: tracemind-instrumentation
updatePolicy: Check tracemind.agent_guidance before changing TraceMind instrumentation. If local Skill, AGENTS rules, or manifest content may be stale, call tracemind.check_agent_setup and merge only missing guidance after telling the user which files will change. In multi-project setups, use tracemind.project_info, availableCapabilities, or MCP tool metadata to confirm the project before adding events. Daily operations review uses dashboard-aligned project_health/recent_online before instrumentation setup; use tracemind.project_info availableCapabilities to find the canonical currentOnline, projectHealth, and deliveryDiagnostics tools, use tracemind.project_health to surface SDK upgrade findings, Web captureScriptFindings, daily health, Dashboard-aligned trends, data retention windows, agent setup update reminders, and current attention items before changing instrumentation, use tracemind.query_delivery_diagnostics for privacy-safe 7-day hourly source/platform, reason class, HTTP status class, queue depth, retry/drop counts, and recovery-duration drilldown without raw errors, request/response bodies, URLs, logs, user content, or session/device/batch identifiers, and use tracemind.recent_online for current online users, real-time users, active pages, and last 30 minutes activity. Treat tracemind.summary totals as sample-derived according to summarySample; do not use summary.totalEvents, topActions, or dailyActiveUsers as full-day totals or current online users. If reporting tools such as tracemind.project_health, tracemind.query_delivery_diagnostics, tracemind.recent_online, tracemind.query_raw_behaviors, or tracemind.submit_feedback are missing from the current active tool list, read MCP tools/list or retry discovery with the exact tool name before concluding they are unavailable; refresh connector/session/MCP config/token and call tracemind.project_info again if they are still missing. Do not compensate for missing current online or project health capabilities by increasing tracemind.summary.limit. If captureScriptFindings appear, call tracemind.capture_setup({ platform: "web" }), replace fixed capture.<hash>.js or self-hosted Web scripts with the returned stable captureScriptUrl snippet, check CDN/service worker/WebView caches, verify window.TraceMind.status().scriptReleaseId, trigger a real event, and re-check project_health. For server deploy verification, use capture_setup projectKeyUsage, configurationModes, preDeployChecks, postDeployVerification, and expectedCaptureQuery; prefer inline public projectKey, use TRACEMIND_PROJECT_KEY only as optional deployment env config, and do not use MCP tokens, Bearer tokens, or TraceMind internal product usage dogfood variables as capture keys. Respect retention windows: capture delivery diagnostics keep 7 days, presence sessions, raw behaviors, and semantic events keep 10 days, and older analysis should use daily/hourly health reports before assuming data loss. Ask the user before updating local skill or instruction files. Ask before calling tracemind.submit_feedback unless the developer explicitly requested submission. If the developer asks whether you can directly feedback to TraceMind, search for and use tracemind.submit_feedback instead of concluding from a partial active tool list that no feedback tool exists. Use captureError for privacy-safe app_error summaries with sanitized previews and irreversible fingerprints, never raw stacks or messages. Use submitFeedback, query_user_feedback, and update_user_feedback for terminal user feedback workflows.
sdkUpgradePolicy: For SDK platforms, capture_setup returns latestSdk, installedVersionDetection, upgradePolicy, upgradeCommands, and verificationCommands. Use registry install commands when distributionMode is registry; use localSourceFallback or local_source instructions only when needed. Fetch source from latestSdk.sourceRef exactly for local-source installs; release builds use immutable tracemind-release-<version> tags instead of floating main. Compare sourceDetails.sdkContentHash against latestSdk.contentHash; displayVersion is only human-readable.
